# SecIdentityCreate(_:_:_:) | Apple Developer Documentation

- [ Security ](/documentation/security)

- [ SecIdentityCreate(_:_:_:) ](/documentation/Security/SecIdentityCreate(_:_:_:))

-  SecIdentityCreate(_:_:_:) 

Function# SecIdentityCreate(_:_:_:)

iOS 11.2+iPadOS 11.2+Mac Catalyst 13.1+macOS 10.12+tvOS 11.2+visionOS 1.0+watchOS 4.2+

```
func SecIdentityCreate(
    _ allocator: CFAllocator?,
    _ certificate: SecCertificate,
    _ privateKey: SecKey
) -> SecIdentity?
```

## [ Parameters ](/documentation/Security/SecIdentityCreate(_:_:_:)#parameters)

`allocator`CFAllocator to allocate the identity object. Pass NULL to use the default allocator.

`certificate`A certificate reference.

`privateKey`A private key reference.

## [Return Value](/documentation/Security/SecIdentityCreate(_:_:_:)#return-value)

An identity reference.

## [Discussion](/documentation/Security/SecIdentityCreate(_:_:_:)#discussion)

Create a new identity object from the provided certificate and its associated private key.

This interface returns null if the private does not key correspond to the public key in the certifcate.
